title: Magically Crispy Malaysian Courgettes Satay
description: A romantic Malaysian dish featuring fermented and crispy courgettes, perfect for a special occasion, this sugar-free-friendly recipe is a delicious twist on traditional satay.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine courgette pieces, salt, and enough water to cover them, then let it sit at room temperature for 24 hours to ferment.
Rinse the fermented courgettes and dry them thoroughly with paper towels.
In a blender, combine coconut milk, rice vinegar, garlic, ginger, coriander seeds, cumin seeds, and turmeric powder, then blend until smooth.
In a large bowl, toss the dried courgettes with the blended marinade until they are evenly coated.
Line a baking sheet with parchment paper and arrange the marinated courgettes in a single layer.
Drizzle the peanut oil over the courgettes and toss to coat.
Bake the courgettes in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, then increase the temperature to 220C and continue baking for an additional 10 minutes, or until crispy and golden brown.
Remove the courgettes from the oven and let them cool slightly before serving.
Serve the crispy courgettes hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
